title: Papillomavirus DNA Extraction from Cervical Brushes Using Wizard® Kit
author: Geórgia ackradt Rêgo

steps.instruction: For the collection of endocervical material, the speculum is inserted into the vaginal canal
After viewing the cervix, the brush is introduced into the cervical canal
Then the brush is rotated 360 degrees by eight turns to the right and eight to the left in order
After collection, the brush is transferred to a clean collection tube and stored at -80 °C
Add 600μl of Nuclei Lysis Solution to a 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube free of DNAses, RNAses and pyrogens
Add the endocervical brush to the tube containing the Nuclei Lysis Solution and homogenize the sample by rotating the brush in the solution
Remove the brush and add 3µl of RNase Solution and mix the sample by inverting the tube 5 times
Incubate the mixture for 30 minutes at 37°C. Allow the sample to cool to room temperature for 5 minutes before proceeding
To the room temperature sample, add 200µl of Protein Precipitation Solution and vortex vigorously at high speed for 20 seconds. Chill sample on ice for 5 minutes
Centrifuge at room temperature at 14,000 x g for 4 minutes
Transfer supernatant to a fresh tube containing 600µl of room temperature 99.5% isopropanol
Mix gently by inversion and centrifuge for 1 minute at 14,000 × g at room temperature
Decant the supernatant and add 600µl of room temperature 70% ethanol, rinsing the tube walls and homogenizing by inversion
Centrifuge for 1 minute at 14,000 × g at room temperature and aspirate the ethanol
Invert the tube on clean absorbent paper, and air-dry the pellet for 15 minutes
Rehydrate the DNA in 100µl of DNA Rehydration Solution for 1 hour at 65°C. Periodically mix the solution by gently tapping the tube
